The Podostemaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the "whip-cord plants" due to their unique growth habits. They are typically found in tropical and subtropical regions, often growing in freshwater environments. The classification of Ledermanniella thollonii is further specified by its kingdom (Plantae), division (Angiospermae), and order (Malpighiales).
